Priority fixes:
- C-001/C-002: Raise text-secondary (#808080→#767676) and text-tertiary
  (#D0D0D0→#767676) to 4.5:1 contrast in light mode. Fix dark mode
  text-tertiary (#4A4A4A→#8A8A8A) which was 1.4:1.

  aria-labelledby referencing the h2 id instead of aria-label={title}.

  have time to read messages.

- A-005: ProgressBar fill div has role="progressbar" with aria-valuenow/
  min/max/label attributes.

  readers announce progress during plan generation.

commit.message:
Fix gaps: missing patterns, families, schemas, and vocabulary mapping

Capabilities added (3):
- selfdestruct.yaml: SELFDESTRUCT opcode detection via opcode_profile
- delegatecall.yaml: DELEGATECALL opcode detection via opcode_profile
- burn.yaml: burn/burnFrom capability detection

Both selfdestruct and delegatecall capabilities include detection_source,
detection_field, and detection_condition metadata so the matcher knows
these come from opcode analysis, not selector/ABI/source matching.

Threat families added (5):
- mint-dump-rug: simplest rug pull (mint + retained ownership, no
  pause/blacklist required)
- anti-whale-trap: maxTx/maxWallet manipulation + fee control
- cooldown-trap: extreme cooldown + secondary exploit vector
- delegatecall-backdoor: hidden delegatecall + mint/fees/helpers
- burn-confiscation: forced burn + re-mint theft

Schemas added (3):
- schema/selector-catalog.schema.yaml
- schema/capability-pattern.schema.yaml
- schema/threat-family.schema.yaml

All three are JSON Schema (draft 2020-12) expressed in YAML for
readability. They define required fields, value constraints, patterns,
and enums for each file type. The threat-family schema includes the
Phase 2 deployer-scoped matching fields.

Documentation added:
- docs/vocabulary.md: maps every valid field in family match.requires
  to its source (capability pattern ID vs state reading field), explains
  how the matcher should resolve each one, and documents the distinction
  between "what a contract can do" (capabilities) and "how it's currently
  configured" (state readings like ownership_renounced).

README updated to reflect all new files.

* [fix] implement --depth flag and auto depth detection for mille init because of PR 11

- Add `ancestor_at_depth(dir, depth)` helper: returns ancestor at target depth or None
  if the dir is shallower (e.g. "src/domain/entity" at depth=2 → "src/domain")

* [fix] disambiguate same-name layers with different parent dirs in mille init because of PR 11

Dirs sharing a base name (e.g. "entity") are now assigned unique layer names when
their immediate parents differ (e.g. domain/entity vs infrastructure/entity):
  - same parent base among all same-name dirs → keep plain "{base}" name (monorepo merge)
  - different parent bases → qualify as "{parent}_{base}" for each group

Key design:
- Grouping is computed across ALL tiers so that dirs in different topological tiers
  but with the same base name are still compared for disambiguation
- allow list now uses the qualified layer name (e.g. "domain_entity") when referencing deps
- Existing monorepo-merge behavior is preserved: apps/crawler/src/domain +
  apps/server/src/domain (both parent "src") still merge into one "domain" layer

Problem: packages/pypi/src/lib.rs had a hand-rolled arg parser that only handled

* ci: add GitHub Actions workflow for lint, type-check, build, and package

Adds .github/workflows/ci.yml that runs on every push to main and claude/**
branches and on pull requests to main. Enforces project standards and
produces a downloadable .vsix artifact for beta distribution.

Steps:
  1. pnpm/action-setup@v4 — installs pnpm 9
  2. actions/setup-node@v4 — pins Node 20.11.0 (matches Volta config)
  3. pnpm install — installs all devDependencies
  4. format:check — prettier --check to verify formatting without modifying files
  5. lint — eslint across src/
  6. typecheck — tsc --noEmit to catch type errors without emitting files
  7. build — esbuild production bundle → dist/extension.js
  8. package — vsce package --no-dependencies → hexfield-text-<version>.vsix
  9. Upload artifact — stored for 30 days, downloadable by any repo collaborator

Also adds two scripts to package.json:
  typecheck: tsc --noEmit   (used by CI step 6 and convenient locally)
  format:check: prettier --check (CI-safe counterpart to format --write)

* fix(ci): fix failing setup-node cache + auto-commit lockfile on first run

Two problems with the previous workflow:

1. `cache: 'pnpm'` in actions/setup-node@v4 requires pnpm-lock.yaml to exist
   in the repo (it hashes it for the cache key). Without it the step errors:
   "Dependencies file is not found ... for pnpm". Removed that option and
   replaced it with explicit pnpm store path caching via actions/cache@v4,
   which gracefully falls back when no lockfile exists yet.

2. No lockfile was committed, so installs were non-deterministic and the store
   cache had no stable key. Added a "Commit lockfile if newly generated" step:
   after the first `pnpm install` on GitHub Actions generates pnpm-lock.yaml,
   the step commits and pushes it back to the branch under github-actions[bot].
   Commits from that bot do not re-trigger CI, so there is no loop. Subsequent
   runs will have a locked, cacheable install.

permissions bumped to contents: write on the job (needed for the bot push).

Two lint/format failures:

1. .eslintrc.json — eslint:recommended enables no-unused-vars as an error.
   When combined with @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars the base rule also
   runs, but it does not understand TypeScript type-only constructs (e.g.
   `type Proximity = ...` used solely in type positions). The standard fix
   is to turn the base rule off and let @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars
   handle everything — it is type-aware and handles the same cases correctly.

2. dueDateDecorator.ts — PROXIMITY_COLORS used hand-aligned comment padding
   (extra spaces before //) to keep the comments in a visual column. Prettier
   normalises all inline comments to a single space, so prettier --check

* fix(lint): add env:node and disable no-undef

eslint:recommended enables no-undef as an error. When combined with the
TypeScript ESLint parser, Node.js globals like setTimeout and clearTimeout
are not always recognised as defined — the scope manager's ambient global
handling is not guaranteed for all TypeScript/tseslint version combinations.

Two targeted fixes (both recommended by the TypeScript ESLint docs):
  - "env": { "node": true } — explicitly tells ESLint that Node.js globals
    (setTimeout, clearTimeout, process, Buffer, etc.) are in scope
  - "no-undef": "off" — TypeScript itself enforces undefined-variable checks
    with stricter accuracy; running the base ESLint rule in a TypeScript
    project is redundant and prone to false positives

https://claude.ai/code/session_01B6NVinrGSL5KzskFxSi31G

* fix(format): collapse setTextDocumentLanguage call to one line

Prettier collapses multi-line function calls when the single-line
version fits within printWidth (100). The call:

  vscode.languages.setTextDocumentLanguage(document, HEXFIELD_LANGUAGE_ID)

is exactly 100 characters at its indentation level, so Prettier keeps
it on one line. The multi-line form I had written did not match Prettier's
output, causing the format:check step to fail.

UI refresh with full bill management and testing (#1)

* Complete UI/UX refresh with full bill management and testing

Redesign all components with mobile-first responsive Tailwind UI using
a warm neutral + indigo accent palette with DM Sans/Fraunces typography.
Wire up full CRUD (add/edit/delete bills), mark paid/unpaid per instance,
summary dashboard stats, dark mode toggle, and page transitions.

Add Vitest (44 unit tests) and Playwright (9 e2e tests) covering
formatting utils, store mutations, component rendering, and full user flows.

Replace mock data with 11 realistic bills using dates relative to today.
Remove unused boilerplate (HelloWorld, SideBar, AboutView).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address PR review: date consistency, strict parsing, real mutations in tests

- Fix markPaid to use day-level date comparison matching checkIfPaid/markUnpaid
- Strict numeric validation for route param id in BillDetailsView
- Parse YYYY-MM-DD query dates as local calendar dates to avoid UTC timezone shift
- E2E mark-paid test now uses hard assertion instead of conditional
- Store unit tests import real mutations instead of reimplementing them

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Guard null dueDate for non-recurring bills, fix flaky e2e selector

- generateBillInstances now skips non-recurring bills with null dueDate
  instead of producing an epoch (1970-01-01) instance
- Replace flaky 'text=Details' e2e selector with '.card h2' which targets
  actual rendered bill name heading

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix end-of-month drift in monthly recurrence generation

setMonth() on dates like Jan 31 overflows into March, causing Feb to be
skipped entirely and subsequent months to drift. Fix by resetting to
day 1 before advancing the month, then clamping dayOfMonth to the
target month's last day (e.g. Jan 31 -> Feb 28 -> Mar 31 -> Apr 30).

Applies to both calculateDueDate and incrementDate. Added unit test
covering the Jan 31 edge case across Feb/Mar/Apr.

commit.message:
feat: Add memory tracking and context simulation to benchmarks

- Add --context-kb option to simulate large LLM context windows
- Track memory_mb_before, memory_mb_after, memory_delta_mb per strategy
- Track GC runs to show garbage collection pressure
- Document Ractor local GC advantage in comments

This demonstrates Ruby 4.x Ractor's key benefit for LLM workloads:
each Ractor has its own garbage collector, so large context allocations
in one Ractor don't trigger stop-the-world pauses for others.

Example with 256KB context per request:
  - Sequential: Δmem=1.4MB
  - Threads: Δmem=1.2MB
  - Ractors: Δmem=0.2MB (7x less memory pressure!)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
fix: weather feed pipeline — real API, field alignment, daemon enabled check (#31)

* fix: comprehensive weather feed pipeline — real API, enabled check, cross-boundary tests

Root cause: Weather feed had 5 independent break points preventing temperature
from displaying in the TUI status line:

1. **BP1 — Daemon skips disabled feeds**: runAllFeeds() didn't check the Enabled
   flag, polling all registered producers regardless of config. Added isEnabled()
   check that skips disabled producers (fail-open for unknown feeds).

2. **BP2 — No weather config in default template**: The init command's YAML
   template had no weather section. Added commented weather config example with
   lat/lon/unit fields.

3. **BP3 — Placeholder producer with wrong field names**: WeatherProducer returned
   hardcoded 0°F with snake_case fields (unit, wind_speed) while Python TUI
   expects camelCase (temperatureUnit, windSpeed). Replaced with real Open-Meteo
   API integration using WMO weather codes, emoji mapping, and fallback caching.

4. **BP4 — ConfigAware interface**: Added ConfigAware interface so producers can
   receive FeedsConfig (latitude, longitude, temperature_unit) before producing.

5. **BP5 — Cross-boundary tests**: Added Go-side bridge test verifying
   FlattenForTUI preserves weather field names, plus 4 Python-side rendering
   tests validating the exact cache shape renders correctly in the TUI.

Also added: isEnabled unit tests, disabled-feed skip test, fallbackResult for
graceful degradation when API is unreachable.

Closes #29

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address CodeRabbit review — shared fixture, nil fallback, config template

- Add temperature_unit to weather config template (CR comment #1)
- Use nil for temperature/weatherCode in fallback when no cached data exists,
  so TUI shows "--" instead of plausible-looking 0°F (CR comment #3)
- Add WeatherTestFixture() shared fixture in feeds package; bridge test uses
  it instead of hardcoded keys, so producer field renames break tests (CR #2)
- Use configured temperatureUnit in fallback (respects celsius config)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(omega-v4): strengthen auto-router — keyword pre-check + bilingual prompt

Problem: Dutch action words like 'automatiseer', 'start' were classified
as PRAAT because the LLM prompt was Dutch-only and the model struggled.

Fix 1: Added _snelle_actie_check() — instant keyword pre-check (0ms)
against 30+ Dutch/English action words. Catches obvious actions without
any API call, bypassing the LLM entirely.

Fix 2: Switched poortwachter prompt to English with Dutch examples.
Now accepts both ACTION and ACTIE in response parsing.

Fix 3: Added visible routing indicators in both MIND and BODY columns:
"Routing: ACTIE → BODY pipeline" / "Routing: PRAAT → MIND stream"
plus "Poortwachter analyseert..." feedback in the live buffer.
